feedback.text: I believe one of the issues not covered in this act is the owning of video games. I am worried that after the game's I enjoy, and paid for through the initial purchase or through microtransactions, will be taken offline with no recourse for me, despite the fact that I have paid for the games I enjoy. In my opinion, when the video games are in their design phase, it is viable to make a change, not requiring an "always online" service, or to use an open source server architecture, where the community could host their own servers in exchange for paying a piece of their own micro transactions to the company that own's the intellectual property. If I, as a consumer, do not own the games I paid for, then that goes again what I believe to be the common consensus of "you buy it, you own it".
